title: Replaced 5 tools with one
review_text: We migrated from Confluence Trello and Google Docs to Notion and haven't looked back. The flexibility of the block system means every team can customize their workspace. Our engineering team uses it for docs and sprint planning while marketing uses it for content calendars.
pros: Incredibly flexible block system; replaces multiple tools; great templates
cons: Steep learning curve for non-technical users; can be slow with large databases

title: Great for small teams but scaling is tricky
review_text: Perfect for our 15-person startup. Wiki docs tasks and notes all in one place. However as we've grown to 50+ people the workspace gets messy fast. Permissions are confusing and finding things requires too many clicks. Need better admin controls.
pros: All-in-one workspace; affordable pricing; great API
cons: Permissions system is confusing; search could be better; no granular admin controls

title: Powerful but overwhelming
review_text: There's a reason people call it the blank page problem. Notion can do everything which means setting it up takes forever. We spent 2 months just designing our workspace structure. Once it's set up it's great but the setup cost is real and significant.
pros: Unlimited customization; great for documentation; solid integrations
cons: Setup is time-consuming; blank page problem; can feel overwhelming for new users

title: Best knowledge management tool we've used
review_text: Our company wiki lives in Notion and it's transformed how we share information. The nested pages and toggle blocks make organizing complex documentation intuitive. Search actually works well now after their recent updates. Onboarding new hires is 10x faster.
pros: Excellent for wikis; nested page structure; good search; toggle blocks
cons: Mobile app is clunky; offline mode is limited; loading can be slow

title: Not enterprise ready
review_text: We tried to roll this out across our 2000-person org and hit serious walls. No SSO on lower tiers. Audit logs are basic. Content governance is nearly impossible. The platform is clearly designed for small teams and startups not enterprise.
pros: Nice UI; good for personal use; flexible templates
cons: No enterprise-grade security; poor audit logging; governance nightmare at scale

title: Game changer for product management
review_text: We use Notion for PRDs roadmaps and sprint planning. The database views are incredibly powerful — switching between table kanban calendar and timeline views of the same data is seamless. Integrations with Slack and Linear work well too.
pros: Database views are excellent; great for PRDs; good integrations
cons: Real-time collaboration can lag; no native Gantt chart; formula syntax is confusing

title: My second brain
review_text: I use Notion for literally everything — personal journal habit tracking reading lists meeting notes project plans. The free plan is incredibly generous. Templates from the community save so much time. Can't imagine life without it now.
pros: Free plan is generous; templates marketplace; works for personal and professional
cons: Syncing between devices can lag; no end-to-end encryption; limited offline

title: Good but not great for project management
review_text: If you need a dedicated project management tool look at Asana Linear or Monday instead. Notion's database kanban boards are nice for light project tracking but lack features like dependencies workload views and automation rules that real PM tools have.
pros: Flexible for light PM; good documentation alongside tasks; customizable
cons: Missing critical PM features; no dependencies; limited automations; no workload view

title: Notion AI is surprisingly useful
review_text: Was skeptical about the AI add-on but it's actually been helpful. Summarizing long docs generating first drafts of specs and translating content for our international team. Not a replacement for real writing but a solid productivity boost.
pros: AI features are practical; good summarization; translation works well
cons: AI costs extra per user; sometimes generates generic output; AI search needs work

title: Transformed our remote team culture
review_text: With our fully remote team spread across 6 time zones Notion became our single source of truth. Async documentation replaced most meetings. The commenting and mention system keeps conversations contextual. Our meeting count dropped 40%.
pros: Async-first design; comments and mentions; reduces meetings; good for remote teams
cons: Time zone handling for databases could improve; notification system is noisy

title: Performance is unacceptable
review_text: Notion is painfully slow. Pages take 3-5 seconds to load. Large databases freeze the browser. The desktop app is just an Electron wrapper that eats RAM. For a tool we use all day every day this kind of performance is a dealbreaker.
pros: Feature-rich; looks nice; good concept
cons: Slow page loads; RAM hungry; Electron app is heavy; databases lag with 1000+ rows

title: Best for technical documentation
review_text: Our engineering team moved all technical docs to Notion and it's been great. Code blocks with syntax highlighting inline databases for tracking API endpoints and the ability to embed anything. Much better than Confluence for modern dev teams.
pros: Code blocks; API documentation; embeds; better than Confluence
cons: Export options are limited; no version diffing; can't self-host

title: Finally a tool everyone actually uses
review_text: We've tried Confluence SharePoint and Coda. Notion is the first tool where adoption wasn't a struggle. The UI is clean and inviting. People actually want to write docs here which is a minor miracle in any organization.
pros: High adoption rate; clean UI; people enjoy using it; low friction
cons: Pricey at scale; limited admin controls on lower tiers; search ranking could improve

title: Good for docs bad for data
review_text: The relational database feature sounds great in theory but in practice it's clunky. Rollup and formula columns are confusing. If you need real database functionality use Airtable. Notion databases are glorified spreadsheets with nice views.
pros: Good for documents and wikis; nice UI for basic data; views are helpful
cons: Database features are shallow; formulas are confusing; no real computed fields

title: Excellent client-facing portal
review_text: We use Notion as a client portal for our agency. Clients love the clean interface and can easily find deliverables status updates and meeting notes. The share-to-web feature is simple. Saves us from building a custom portal.
pros: Share to web is great; clean client-facing view; easy for non-tech clients
cons: Limited branding on shared pages; analytics on shared pages would be nice; no forms on free plan

title: Student essential tool
review_text: As a grad student Notion keeps my life organized. Research papers class notes thesis planning reading lists — everything connected through databases and backlinks. The student plan discount makes it even better. Couldn't survive grad school without it.
pros: Student discount; backlinks; great for research; databases connect everything
cons: Can be distracting to set up; easy to over-engineer systems; mobile editing is awkward

title: Customer support is terrible
review_text: When we had a billing issue it took 3 weeks to get a response. When our workspace had a sync issue that corrupted some pages the support team just sent canned responses. For a paid product the support experience is shockingly bad.
pros: Product itself is good; nice UI; useful features
cons: Support response time is awful; canned responses; no phone support; no SLA

title: Best meeting notes system we've built
review_text: Our entire org uses Notion for meeting notes linked to project databases. Action items from meetings automatically appear in team task boards. It took time to set up but the connected workflow is powerful and keeps everyone accountable.
pros: Connected databases for meetings and tasks; templates for consistency; good for accountability
cons: Template setup takes time; some team members resist adopting; notification overload

title: Notion is our company operating system
review_text: From employee handbook to OKRs to sprint boards to product specs — everything runs through Notion. It's become our company OS. The ability to connect any page to any database means information flows naturally instead of living in silos.
pros: True all-in-one platform; connects information across teams; replaces multiple subscriptions
cons: Single point of failure risk; export should be easier; vendor lock-in concerns

title: Good tool wrong pricing model
review_text: The per-seat pricing gets expensive fast. At $10/user/month for a team of 100 that's $12K/year for what is essentially a wiki and task manager. Confluence is cheaper at scale. The free plan is great but the jump to paid plans is steep for larger teams.
pros: Feature-rich; good free plan; nice UI
cons: Per-seat pricing scales poorly; expensive for large teams; feature gaps vs dedicated tools
